error.signature: AI tells a renter in Japan that upfront costs are just first month + deposit, like in the US/UK
error.regex: (japan.*rent|tokyo.*apartment|japan.*key money|shikikin|reikin|敷金|礼金|日本.*家賃|guarantor japan|保証人.*日本|japan.*apartment.*deposit)

verdict.summary: Japanese rental upfront costs typically total 4–6 months of rent: 敷金 (shikikin, security deposit, 1–2 months, returned minus damages), 礼金 (reikin, 'key money' gift to landlord, 1–2 months, NON-REFUNDABLE), first month rent, brokerage fee (1 month + tax), guarantor-company fee (0.5–1 month), fire insurance (~20,000 JPY/2 years), lock change (~15,000 JPY). Some foreigner-friendly agencies (Leopalace, Sakura House, GaijinPot) waive reikin and guarantor.

dead_ends.action: Plan a Tokyo 100,000 JPY/month rental with a 200,000 JPY budget (first + deposit)
dead_ends.why_fails: Actual move-in on a 100,000 JPY/month apartment: ~500,000–700,000 JPY (5–7 months rent-equivalent). Components: shikikin 100-200k (refundable), reikin 100-200k (gift), first month 100k, brokerage 100k+tax, guarantor fee 50-100k, insurance 20k, lock 15k. Sudden realization at contract signing is a common expat shock.

dead_ends.action: Tell a foreigner they just need to fill out a rental application, skipping the guarantor system
dead_ends.why_fails: Most Japanese rentals require a 保証人 (hoshounin, personal guarantor — Japanese national with stable income) OR a 保証会社 (hoshou-gaisha, guarantor company, ~0.5–1 month rent). Foreigners without a Japanese guarantor must use a guarantor company, and many landlords additionally refuse foreigners even with a company (the 'no foreigners' 外国人お断り clause is legal and common in older buildings).

dead_ends.action: Expect to break the lease without penalty after 3–6 months like in the US
dead_ends.why_fails: Japanese leases are typically 2-year renewable. Early termination often triggers: 1–2 month penalty fee + forfeit of shikikin for 'damages' assessed at move-out + possible cleaning fee. Mid-contract breakage with full refund requires documented landlord breach.

workarounds.action: Budget 5–6 months of rent for move-in. Use foreigner-specific agencies (Sakura House, Metro Residence, GaijinPot Apartments) or no-reikin/no-shikikin 'zero-zero' listings (ゼロゼロ物件) — often slightly higher monthly rent but vastly lower upfront.

Use a guarantor company (保証会社) — many agencies bundle this automatically. Fee is one-time ~50-100% of monthly rent. Alternatively, monthly serviced apartments (Monthly Mansion) skip the system entirely but cost 2-3× a normal lease for equivalent space.

Move-out: request an itemized damage assessment (原状回復). Natural wear (wall discoloration from sun, faint marks) should be landlord-covered per MLIT guidelines. Scuffs, burns, stains deducted from shikikin. Disputes can go to the local consumer center (国民生活センター).
